method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting). Portal is Microsoft Dynamics 365; probed the Open311 GeoReport v2 endpoints (retired), the api.nyc.gov gateway (401, GeoClient only), and the 311 dataset family on NYC Open Data (agency OTI).
headline_findings: Fifth distinct platform: Microsoft Dynamics 365 / Power Apps Portals on Azure — the city's front door is a vendor CRM.
The 311 data is the most-used in the city: 311 Service Requests (erm2-nwe9) has 1.26M views / 590k downloads and a live SODA endpoint over tens of millions of records.
But the interactive service API is retired: NYC was an early Open311 (GeoReport v2) adopter and the standard endpoints (311api.cityofnewyork.us) no longer resolve.
A real API gateway exists (api.nyc.gov, Azure APIM) but is scoped to GeoClient geocoding and key-gated (401) — not 311.
5-for-5 on the universal gap: submitting/tracking a 311 request has no public API (Open311 lapsed; Dynamics has no public write API).

apis_observed.note: GeoClient geocoding (401); nearest thing to a central NYC gateway.

fruit.name: 311 Service Requests
fruit.notes: The core object. Fully machine-readable as OPEN DATA (Open311 'request' shape). But submitting/tracking via API is not public (Open311 retired).

fruit.name: Report a Problem (submit)
fruit.notes: Create a service request. Runs on Dynamics 365; no public write API. Open311 POST /requests once covered this and is retired. The net-new (re-standardize) write surface.

fruit.name: Service Catalog (complaint/service types)
fruit.notes: The catalog of complaint types/descriptors = Open311 'services' list.

fruit.name: Service Level Agreements (by type)
fruit.notes: Expected resolution time per complaint type.

fruit.name: Responding Agencies
fruit.notes: The agency that owns each request type (DSNY, DOT, NYPD, HPD…).

fruit.name: Satisfaction & call-center metrics
fruit.notes: Operational/experience metrics — all on Open Data.
